quantification_methods: MS1 intensity based label-free quantification method

publications.ref: Grassl N, Kulak NA, Pichler G, Geyer PE, Jung J, Schubert S, Sinitcyn P, Cox J, Mann M. Ultra-deep and quantitative saliva proteome reveals dynamics of the oral microbiome. Genome Med. 2016 Apr 21;8(1):44

Characteristics[Organism].evidence: Standard clinical cotton swabs were used to collect saliva form eight healthy individuals

Characteristics[OrganismPart].evidence: Standard clinical cotton swabs were used to collect saliva form eight healthy individuals

Characteristics[MaterialType].evidence: simultaneously characterize the human and microbiome contributions to the proteome of a body fluid

Characteristics[Label].evidence: MS1 intensity based label-free quantification method confirmed from PRIDE database and quantitative measurements described in abstract

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: Standard clinical cotton swabs were used to collect saliva form eight healthy individuals

Characteristics[Disease].reasoning: The text mentions 'eight healthy individuals', suggesting no disease condition
Characteristics[Disease].needs: Confirmation whether 'healthy' should be recorded as disease status or if this category should be not_applicable

STEP1_OVERVIEW.summary: This is a shotgun proteomics study of human saliva collected from healthy individuals using cotton swabs. The study uses label-free quantification and aims to characterize both human and microbial proteins in saliva. Key uncertainties remain around whether the healthy status of subjects should be recorded as a disease characteristic or considered not applicable.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: a total of 20 μg of protein was digested by adding 0.4 μg trypsin and LysC to our lysis buffer and incubating for 60 min at 37 °C while shaking

Characteristics[AlkylationReagent].evidence: lysis buffer (1 % sodium dodecyl carbonate (v/v), 10 mM tris (2-carboxyethyl) phosphine, 40 mM 2-chloroacetamide, 100 mM Tris buffer pH 8.5)

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: lysis buffer (1 % sodium dodecyl carbonate (v/v), 10 mM tris (2-carboxyethyl) phosphine, 40 mM 2-chloroacetamide, 100 mM Tris buffer pH 8.5)

Characteristics[Label].evidence: Relative quantification was performed using the default parameters of the MaxLFQ algorithm

Characteristics[Modification].evidence: We set N-acetylation of proteins' N-termini (42.010565 Da) and oxidation of methionine (15.994915 Da) as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].1.evidence: We set N-acetylation of proteins' N-termini (42.010565 Da) and oxidation of methionine (15.994915 Da) as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].2.evidence: carbamidomethylation of cysteine as a fixed modification (57.021464 Da)

Comment[FractionationMethod].evidence: we used basic reversed phase chromatography to fractionate our eight waking samples prior to liquid chromatography (LC)-MS measurement

Comment[Separation].evidence: Approximately 15 μg of peptides were separated in an 80-min gradient on a 20-cm, 75-μm inner diameter column that was in-house packed with ReproSil-Pur C18 beads

Comment[NumberOfFractions].reasoning: Text mentions fractionating 'eight waking samples' and 'concatenated fractions' but doesn't explicitly state the number of fractions created
Comment[NumberOfFractions].needs: Clear statement of how many fractions were generated from the basic reversed phase chromatography

STEP2_SAMPLE_PREP.summary: Sample preparation details are well documented: trypsin/LysC digestion with TCEP reduction and 2-chloroacetamide alkylation. Three modifications identified: N-terminal acetylation and methionine oxidation (variable), carbamidomethylation of cysteine (fixed). Basic reversed phase fractionation was used for deep proteome analysis, though the exact number of fractions remains unclear. No enrichment, depletion, or synthetic peptide spikes were mentioned.

Comment[Instrument].evidence: The chromatography was on-line coupled to a Q Exactive HF mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific)

Comment[FragmentationMethod].evidence: The 15 most intense ions per MS scan were selected for higher-energy collisional dissociation (HCD) fragmentation

Comment[IonizationType].evidence: by applying a spray voltage of 2.2 kV

Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer].evidence: measured at a resolution of 30,000 (Q Exactive HF uses orbitrap analyzer)

Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance].evidence: The initial allowed mass tolerance was set to 4.5 ppm at the MS level

Comment[FragmentMassTolerance].evidence: The initial allowed mass tolerance was set to 4.5 ppm at the MS level and 0.5 Da at the MS/MS level

Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages].evidence: Only tryptic peptides that were at least seven amino acids in length with up to two missed cleavages were considered

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: Both the fractionated and the single run samples were subjected to a 100-min chromatography gradient

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].reasoning: Text mentions 'The 15 most intense ions per MS scan were selected for higher-energy collisional dissociation' which indicates data-dependent acquisition, but the term DDA is not explicitly stated
Comment[AcquisitionMethod].needs: Explicit mention of DDA or data-dependent acquisition method

Comment[CollisionEnergy].reasoning: HCD fragmentation is confirmed but specific collision energy values are not mentioned in the text
Comment[CollisionEnergy].needs: Explicit collision energy values or NCE settings

STEP3_MS_SETTINGS.summary: Mass spectrometry parameters are well documented: Q Exactive HF instrument with HCD fragmentation, electrospray ionization, and orbitrap MS2 analyzer. Search parameters include 4.5 ppm precursor tolerance, 0.5 Da fragment tolerance, up to 2 missed cleavages, and 100-min gradient time. The acquisition method appears to be DDA based on ion selection strategy, but this needs confirmation. Collision energy and flow rate information are not provided in the text.

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic.evidence: We collected saliva at two different time points from four female and four male, healthy, non-smoking individuals aged 24 to 40 years

Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.evidence: Because this showed very high reproducibility (mean R2 = 0.92, Additional file 1: Figure S3b), we did not perform technical replicates in this study

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples].evidence: 8 individuals × 2 time points (waking and postprandial) = 16 single run samples, as confirmed by 'all 16 single shot runs' in the text

Comment[FractionIdentifier].evidence: Fraction numbers are clearly indicated in filenames with 'fractionated[1-8]' and 'frac[1-8]' patterns. Text confirms 'we used basic reversed phase chromatography to fractionate our eight waking samples'

Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].evidence: Subject numbers 1-8 are clearly indicated in filenames. Files with 'm' suffix (e.g., _1m, _2m) represent the same subjects at different time points (postprandial vs waking)

FactorValue[FractionIdentifier].evidence: Same as Comment[FractionIdentifier] - fraction numbers clearly indicated in filenames

FactorValue[Treatment].reasoning: Files with 'm' suffix appear to represent postprandial samples (10 a.m. collection after breakfast), while files without 'm' represent waking samples (immediately after waking). Text mentions 'The first collection was immediately after waking' and 'The second collection took place at 10 a.m., at least 30 min after the donors had eaten breakfast'
FactorValue[Treatment].needs: Confirmation that 'm' suffix specifically indicates postprandial/morning samples vs waking samples

STEP4_RAW_FILES.summary: File analysis confirms 8 biological replicates with no technical replicates, totaling 16 single-run samples. Fractionated samples clearly show 8 fractions per sample based on filename patterns. The 'm' suffix in filenames likely indicates postprandial samples vs waking samples, representing the two time points mentioned in the experimental design. Workflow reproducibility files (rep1, rep2, rep3) and plasma comparison files (PhGe) are separate validation experiments.
